paper_title: Pleistocene and Holocene Mammal Communities on the Edwards Plateau

methods.description: Reconstruction of Pleistocene and Holocene mammal communities on the Edwards Plateau using faunal lists from Hall's Cave and surrounding localities.

steps.description: Reconstruct the Pleistocene and Holocene mammal communities based on faunal lists from Hall's Cave and surrounding localities.

steps.description: Combine all equid species at the generic level due to unsettled taxonomic status.

methods.description: Quantification of site-specific body size using fossils to estimate body mass for animals within the community.

steps.description: Measure molar and postcranial elements from fossils housed at TMM.
steps.searchable_summary: measure fossil elements for body size estimation

steps.description: Use allometric regressions to translate measurements into body mass estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: apply allometric regressions to estimate body mass

methods.description: Measurement of carbon, nitrogen, and oxygen isotope values from fossils to infer dietary patterns.

steps.description: Measure isotope values of fossils from Hall's Cave and surrounding sites.
steps.searchable_summary: measure isotope values from fossil samples

steps.description: Compute 'collagen equivalent' for enamel-derived values using spacing data.

steps.description: Use ANOVAs with Bonferroni corrections to check for biases in isotope data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA to validate isotope data

methods.description: Statistical analysis of isotope values to assess dietary patterns and community-scale changes over time.

outputs: mean, median, SD of isotope values
